title: Natural Stomach Pain Remedies: 7 Best Herbal Creams

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Gingerol is good for stomach pain because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and ease discomfort in the stomach, providing quick relief from nausea and other digestive issues.
Shogaol is good for stomach pain because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and pain in the stomach and intestines.
Curcumenol is good for stomach pain because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and irritation in the stomach lining.
Zingerone is good for stomach pain because it helps reduce inflammation and relaxes the muscles in the stomach, which can ease discomfort and pain.

Gingerols are good for stomach pain because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and ease irritation in the digestive tract, providing quick relief from stomach discomfort.

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale, also known as ginger, has been used for many years to help with stomach pain. The root of the ginger plant contains a special compound that helps to reduce inflammation in the stomach and intestines. When you have stomach pain, it's usually because your stomach is inflamed or irritated. The ginger compound, called gingerol, helps to calm down this inflammation and reduce the pain. It also helps to slow down the movement of food through your digestive system, which can help to prevent nausea and vomiting. Additionally, ginger has natural anti-inflammatory properties that can help to soothe the stomach lining and reduce discomfort. When you use ginger cream on your stomach, the active ingredients are absorbed into your skin and start working right away to help ease your pain.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make an aloe vera cream that helps with stomach pain, start by gathering a few simple ingredients. You will need aloe barbadensis gel, coconut oil, beeswax, vitamin E oil, and a small amount of essential oil like peppermint or ginger. Begin by melting the coconut oil and beeswax in a double boiler or a microwave-safe bowl in short intervals, stirring between each heating to avoid burning. Once the mixture is smooth and melted, remove it from the heat and let it cool for a few minutes. Next, add the aloe vera gel and vitamin E oil to the mixture and stir well. If desired, add a few drops of peppermint or ginger essential oil to help soothe the stomach. Pour the mixture into a clean container and let it cool completely before use. Once set, apply the cream to the stomach area as needed to help reduce pain and discomfort. Be sure to do a patch test before using the cream on a larger area to ensure you are not sensitive to any of the ingredients.
remedies.image_alt: Zingiber officinale herbal creams for stomach pain

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Flavonoids help reduce stomach pain by calming inflammation and soothing the digestive tract, which prevents stomach acid from causing discomfort and irritation.
Saponins are good for stomach pain because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe and calm the digestive system, reducing irritation and discomfort.
Triterpenoids are good for stomach pain because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and ease pain in the stomach.

Glycyrrhizin is good for stomach pain because it reduces inflammation in the digestive system, soothes the stomach lining, and helps to relax the muscles that cause cramps, making it a natural remedy for relieving stomach discomfort.

remedies.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra cream is a natural remedy that can help relieve stomach pain. This cream is made from the root of the licorice plant, which has been used for centuries to soothe digestive issues. The active ingredient, glycyrrhizin, has anti-inflammatory properties that can reduce swelling in the stomach and intestines, leading to pain relief. It also helps to protect the stomach lining from acid damage, which can cause stomach pain and discomfort. Additionally, glycyrrhizin has a soothing effect on the muscles in the stomach, helping to calm spasms and reduce cramps. When applied topically as a cream, glycyrrhizin is absorbed quickly into the skin and then into the bloodstream, where it can work to reduce stomach pain and discomfort. Regular use of Glycyrrhiza glabra cream may help to reduce stomach pain and discomfort caused by conditions such as ulcers, gastritis, and irritable bowel syndrome.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Aloe barbadensis herbal creams that help with stomach pain, you will need aloe vera gel, coconut oil, beeswax, olive oil, vitamin E oil, and a small saucepan. First, mix 1/2 cup of aloe vera gel with 1/4 cup of coconut oil in a bowl, stirring well. Then, melt 2 tablespoons of beeswax in a small saucepan over low heat. Next, add 2 tablespoons of olive oil and 1 teaspoon of vitamin E oil to the melted beeswax, stirring until well combined. Once the mixture has cooled slightly, slowly pour it into the aloe vera mixture, stirring constantly until it thickens. Pour the mixture into a small tin or jar and let it cool completely. This cream can be applied topically to the stomach area to help soothe and calm stomach pain. However, please note that it's essential to do a patch test before using this cream, and consult a healthcare professional if you have any underlying medical conditions.
remedies.image_alt: Glycyrrhiza glabra herbal creams for stomach pain

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Rosmarinic acid helps with stomach pain because it reduces inflammation and relaxes the stomach muscles, allowing it to heal and function properly.
Menthol is good for stomach pain because it helps relax the muscles in the stomach and reduce inflammation, making it a natural remedy for soothing stomach discomfort and cramps.
Luteolin is good for stomach pain because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and relax the muscles in the stomach, relieving discomfort and pain.
Quercetin is good for stomach pain because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and ease pain in the digestive tract.
Menthone is good for stomach pain because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce swelling and ease irritation in the stomach lining.

remedies.remedy_desc: Mentha x piperita, also known as peppermint, has been used for centuries to help with stomach pain. The active ingredient in peppermint creams, called menthol, is what makes them effective. When menthol is applied to the skin, it is absorbed into the body and can help to relax the muscles in the stomach and intestines. This relaxation can help to reduce cramps and spasms that can cause stomach pain. Additionally, peppermint's cooling sensation can help to reduce inflammation and ease nausea. When applied to the stomach area, peppermint creams can also help to ease digestion and reduce bloating, which can contribute to stomach discomfort. The menthol in peppermint creams can also help to block pain signals to the brain, making it a potential natural remedy for stomach pain relief.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Aloe barbadensis herbal cream for stomach pain, start by extracting the gel from a fresh Aloe barbadensis leaf. Cut off the outer green skin and scoop out the clear gel inside. Mix the gel with 1/4 cup of coconut oil in a blender until smooth. Next, add 2 tablespoons of beeswax and 1 tablespoon of shea butter to the blender. Blend until the beeswax and shea butter are fully incorporated. Then, add a few drops of peppermint essential oil to help soothe stomach cramps. Stir in 1 tablespoon of chamomile oil to reduce inflammation. Pour the mixture into a saucepan and heat it on low heat, stirring constantly, until the beeswax has melted. Remove from heat and let it cool until it reaches a thick, creamy consistency. Pour the cream into a clean container and let it cool completely before applying it to the stomach area to help relieve pain and discomfort.
remedies.image_alt: Mentha x piperita herbal creams for stomach pain

Tannins are good for stomach pain because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe and calm the stomach lining, reducing irritation and discomfort.
Foeniculin is good for stomach pain because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and ease digestive discomfort.

remedies.remedy_desc: Foeniculum vulgare, also known as fennel, is a plant that has been used for centuries to help with stomach problems. The creams made from fennel are good for stomach pain because they contain a compound called anethole. Anethole is a natural gas that helps to relax the muscles in the stomach and reduce inflammation. When you apply fennel cream to your stomach, it can help to calm down the muscles and reduce the pain. Fennel cream also has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to reduce swelling in the stomach. This can be especially helpful if you have stomach cramps or digestive issues like bloating or gas. The soothing and relaxing effects of fennel cream can also help to calm down other symptoms like nausea and diarrhea.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Aloe barbadensis herbal creams for stomach pain, start by gathering a few simple ingredients. You'll need aloe vera gel, coconut oil, olive oil, beeswax, shea butter, vitamin E oil, and essential oil like peppermint or lavender. Begin by extracting the gel from the aloe vera leaf by cutting off the top and bottom, then peeling the skin and scooping out the clear gel. Mix 1 part aloe vera gel with 2 parts coconut oil, and 1 part olive oil in a bowl. Melt the beeswax in a double boiler or in a microwave-safe bowl for 10-15 seconds, then stir it into the oil mixture until well combined. Add in 1 tablespoon of shea butter and 1 teaspoon of vitamin E oil, stirring until smooth. Finally, add a few drops of your chosen essential oil and mix well. Pour the mixture into a container and let it cool and set before using as a topical cream to help soothe stomach pain.
remedies.image_alt: Foeniculum vulgare herbal creams for stomach pain

Tannins are good for stomach pain because they can help reduce inflammation and ease digestive issues by slowing down the movement of food through the digestive system, giving the stomach a chance to heal and relax.

remedies.remedy_desc: Curcuma longa cream, also known as turmeric cream, is a natural remedy for stomach pain. It contains curcumin, a powerful compound that reduces inflammation in the digestive system. When we eat, our body breaks down food into smaller parts. Sometimes, these parts can irritate the stomach lining and cause pain. Curcumin in turmeric cream helps to calm down the irritation and inflammation, allowing the stomach lining to heal. This cream also has anti-inflammatory properties, which means it helps to reduce swelling and discomfort in the stomach. As a result, people who use turmeric cream for stomach pain often find relief from cramps, bloating, and discomfort. The cream is also easy to apply and can be used as needed, making it a convenient option for those looking for a natural remedy. Curcuma longa cream can be used after meals or when symptoms occur, and it's best to consult with a doctor before using it regularly.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Aloe barbadensis herbal cream that helps with stomach pain, you'll need a few simple ingredients. First, you'll need pure Aloe barbadensis gel, which you can get from an Aloe plant or buy it at a health store. You'll also need a base oil like coconut oil or olive oil, and a few drops of essential oils like peppermint or ginger, which are known to soothe the stomach. Next, combine 2 tablespoons of Aloe gel with 2 tablespoons of base oil in a bowl, and mix well until they're fully incorporated. Then, add 5-7 drops of your chosen essential oil and mix again. Transfer the mixture to a clean container and store it in the fridge. When you need to use it, simply take a small amount and apply it to your stomach area. The Aloe gel will help to soothe and calm the stomach, while the essential oils will provide a gentle, natural pain relief. You can also add a pinch of baking soda to the mixture to help neutralize any acidity in the stomach, making it a great remedy for heartburn and indigestion.
remedies.image_alt: Curcuma longa herbal creams for stomach pain

Tannins are good for stomach pain because they help reduce inflammation in the stomach lining and soothe the digestive tract, which eases pain and discomfort.

remedies.remedy_desc: Cinchona officinalis is a plant with a long history of use in treating various health issues, including stomach pain. The active compound in this plant, quinine, has anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe the stomach and reduce pain. When applied topically in the form of a cream, quinine from Cinchona officinalis can help ease cramps and discomfort caused by menstrual cramps, indigestion, or other stomach problems. The anti-inflammatory effect of quinine can also help reduce swelling and redness in the stomach area, providing relief from pain and discomfort. This plant-based cream is a natural alternative to over-the-counter medications and can be used in combination with other treatments to manage stomach pain. Regular use of Cinchona officinalis cream may also help in reducing the frequency and severity of stomach cramps over time.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Aloe barbadensis herbal cream that helps with stomach pain, start by gathering the necessary ingredients. You will need 2 ounces of aloe vera gel, which can be extracted from the leaves of the Aloe barbadensis plant or purchased at a health food store. Combine the aloe vera gel with 2 ounces of coconut oil, 1 ounce of shea butter, and 1 ounce of beeswax in a double boiler. Heat the mixture over low heat, stirring occasionally, until the beeswax has melted and the mixture is smooth. Remove the mixture from the heat and let it cool slightly. Next, add 5 drops of peppermint essential oil and 5 drops of ginger essential oil, which have natural anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe stomach pain. Stir well to combine and pour the mixture into a small container. Let the mixture cool and harden completely before using it as a topical cream to help alleviate stomach pain. Apply a small amount to the abdomen and massage gently to help reduce discomfort and inflammation.
remedies.image_alt: Cinchona officinalis herbal creams for stomach pain

remedies.remedy_desc: Aloe barbadensis cream is helpful for stomach pain because it has soothing properties. The gel from the aloe plant has anti-inflammatory ingredients that reduce swelling in the stomach and intestines. This swelling can cause pain and discomfort. When you apply aloe cream to your stomach, the anti-inflammatory ingredients calm the swelling and ease the pain. Aloe also has antispasmodic properties, which help to relax the muscles in the stomach and prevent spasms. This can help to relieve cramps and other painful symptoms. Aloe cream can also help to heal and protect the lining of the stomach, which can become irritated and inflamed due to stomach pain. This can help to prevent further discomfort and promote healing.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Aloe barbadensis herbal cream for stomach pain, start by preparing the aloe vera gel. Cut a mature aloe vera leaf and carefully remove the thick outer skin to expose the clear gel inside. Use a spoon or scoop to extract the gel and place it in a blender or food processor. Add a small amount of coconut oil, shea butter, and beeswax to the blender, and mix until the ingredients are smooth and well combined. Next, add a few drops of peppermint essential oil, which has natural anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe stomach pain. Transfer the mixture to a saucepan and heat it over low heat, stirring constantly, until the mixture is smooth and has a creamy consistency. Remove the saucepan from the heat and let the mixture cool before transferring it to a clean container. Once cooled, the cream is ready to use. To apply, gently massage a small amount onto the stomach area, where the stomach pain is felt, and repeat as needed.
remedies.image_alt: Aloe barbadensis herbal creams for stomach pain

intro_desc: Herbal creams can be a helpful remedy for stomach pain due to the soothing properties of certain herbs. Zingiber officinale, also known as ginger, has natural anti-inflammatory compounds that can reduce inflammation and ease discomfort in the stomach. This can be especially helpful for people who suffer from stomach ulcers or acid reflux. Glycyrrhiza glabra, also known as licorice root, has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that can also help to soothe and calm the stomach.  Using herbal creams that contain these herbs can provide relief from stomach pain by reducing inflammation and promoting healing. If stomach pain is left untreated, it can lead to more serious complications such as digestive problems, nausea, and vomiting. These issues can have a significant impact on daily life, making it difficult to work, socialize, or even enjoy meals. By addressing stomach pain with herbal creams, individuals can improve their quality of life, regain their appetite, and enjoy their favorite foods without discomfort. This can also lead to better sleep, improved mood, and increased energy levels.
supplementary_best: To remedy stomach pain, a combination of herbal creams that work well together is Zingiber officinale (Ginger), Foeniculum vulgare (Fennel), and Aloe barbadensis (Aloe Vera). Zingiber officinale is a natural anti-inflammatory that reduces pain and inflammation in the stomach. Foeniculum vulgare helps to calm the digestive system and ease cramps, while also relieving gas and bloating. Aloe barbadensis soothes the stomach lining, reducing irritation and discomfort. This combination is effective because Zingiber officinale and Foeniculum vulgare address the pain and digestive issues, while Aloe barbadensis provides a protective barrier to prevent further irritation.
supplementary_avoid: If you have stomach pain, it's best to avoid herbal creams that contain certain herbs that can irritate your stomach further. One such herb is 'Achyranthes aspera', also known as prickly chaff flower. It can cause stomach upset, nausea, and diarrhea in some people. Another herb to be cautious with is 'Cassia auriculata', or the ox ear. It can irritate the stomach lining and worsen stomach pain. 'Cassia tora', or the chestnut, is also not recommended, as it can cause stomach upset, nausea, and vomiting. 'Centella asiatica', or Indian pennywort, should be used with caution as it can cause stomach cramps and diarrhea in some individuals. Lastly, 'Rauvolfia serpentina', or the Indian snakeroot, should be avoided as it can cause stomach upset, nausea, and vomiting. Always consult with a healthcare professional before using any herbal cream, especially if you have a pre-existing stomach condition or are taking medications.
supplementary_other_remedies: Ginger tea has been used for centuries to help with stomach pain. The active compounds in ginger, such as gingerol and shogaol, help to reduce inflammation and relax the muscles in the stomach, which can become tight and painful due to digestive issues. Drinking ginger tea can also help to stimulate digestion and improve the movement of food through the digestive system. This can be especially helpful for people who experience bloating and constipation.  Fennel seeds have also been used to alleviate stomach pain. The oils in fennel seeds have a calming effect on the digestive system and can help to reduce inflammation and spasms in the stomach. Fennel seeds can be made into a tea by steeping them in hot water or added to food for extra flavor and relief. Fennel seeds are also a natural carminative, which means they can help to release trapped gas and improve digestion.  Aloe vera juice has been used to soothe stomach pain and inflammation. The antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds in aloe vera help to calm the digestive system and reduce inflammation in the stomach. Drinking aloe vera juice can also help to improve digestion and reduce symptoms of irritable bowel syndrome (IBS). Aloe vera juice is particularly helpful for people who experience acid reflux and heartburn.  Apple cider vinegar has been used to help with stomach pain and digestion. The acetic acid in apple cider vinegar helps to stimulate digestion and improve the movement of food through the digestive system. Apple cider vinegar can also help to reduce inflammation and kill bacteria that can cause stomach pain and digestive issues. Drinking apple cider vinegar mixed with water can be especially helpful for people who experience bloating and constipation.
supplementary_related_ailments: People with stomach pain often experience other health issues due to inflammation and digestive problems. Those with stomach pain may have irritable bowel syndrome (IBS), which causes bloating, diarrhea, and constipation. The medicinal herb Peppermint can help with IBS by reducing inflammation and relaxing the muscles in the digestive tract.  Stomach pain can also lead to acid reflux, where stomach acid flows up into the esophagus, causing heartburn and discomfort. Licorice root can soothe acid reflux by protecting the mucous lining of the stomach and reducing inflammation.  Stomach pain may also be a symptom of gastritis, an inflammation of the stomach lining. The medicinal herb Slippery elm can help heal gastritis by forming a protective barrier on the stomach lining, allowing it to heal and reducing inflammation.  Stomach pain can also be a symptom of stress and anxiety, which can worsen digestive problems. The medicinal herb Chamomile can help reduce stress and anxiety by promoting relaxation and reducing inflammation.  People with stomach pain may also experience fatigue, as the body's energy is redirected to heal the digestive system. The medicinal herb Ginseng can help increase energy and reduce fatigue by stimulating the immune system and reducing inflammation.
